reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome real download+run on WASM, Node cross-check, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser run (headless Chrome, WASM, fresh profile → 'Download model (~280 MB)' → click → auto-run): the model reranked the mixed EN/ZH/ES default set and reordered it — top result #1 = (was #6) 'Succulents and cacti tolerate weeks of neglect, so they are ideal if you travel often.' logit -1.85, sigmoid 13.6% — the genuinely most relevant passage promoted from position 6 to #1. 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Node cross-check (jinaai/jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual, q8, model_type injected): logits [N,1]; on the jina canonical example the two skincare-relevant docs (Chinese + English) ranked top over makeup distractors; within EACH of EN/ES/ZH/DE the relevant doc outranked its same-language irrelevant one; and a code test ranked 'def factorial(n): …' #1 over a SQL query and a cake recipe. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify/jina-reranker-v2/{desktop-light,desktop-dark,mobile-light,mobile-dark}.png
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real WASM run in the browser; cross-lingual AND code reranking confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Cross-encoder output shape is one relevance logit per (query,passage) pair (num_labels=1 → logits [N,1]); higher = more relevant. Semantics correct, cross-lingual AND cross-modal: a Chinese passage ranked #1 for an English query, and code snippets are ranked correctly against a natural-language request. Verified in browser + Node.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (jinaai/jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual, linked), task (text-classification used as a cross-encoder reranker), backend (WebAssembly), quantisation (q8), and the honest loading note (ONNX config omits model_type → inject 'xlm-roberta' + AutoModelForSequenceClassification; no remote code). Inference runs in a module Web Worker (models/jina-reranker-v2/worker.js). Conformance derives declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation + inference-off-main-thread.
rubric.notes: The custom-config loading path is documented on the page, not hidden.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). Headless fresh profile showed 'Download model (~280 MB)'; after the click it auto-loaded to state=ready and ran. PASS 2 (same profile, cached): auto-initialised to state=ready with the action button = 'Clear cached model' (NOT Download). Verified download→ready→run AND cached auto-init in headless.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified fresh-profile download + cached auto-init with no Load button.

rubric.evidence: A role=status aria-live #runStatus region reports 'Scoring query–passage pairs…' / 'Done.' / errors; the shared loader streams download progress and offers Retry on failure; the WASM path needs no GPU so it works offline once cached. Failures surface on the page ('Reranking failed: …'), not only the console.
rubric.notes: Runs on WASM — no WebGPU required; degrades honestly.

rubric.evidence: Query input (Enter reranks), passages textarea, and the Rerank button are real, labelled, keyboard-operable; the use-case pages add scenario chips (multilingual RAG + code/tool selection) and a shortlist top-k number input. All exercised. The results list, before/after view, and cross-encoder-vs-lexical contrast render from the real run.

The ranking, logits, sigmoid probabilities and before/after view are all produced by the on-device cross-encoder run (verified real output above). The lexical-overlap column is clearly labelled as a naive contrast baseline, never presented as the model's ranking. No canned result.

Buttons expose accessible names; the query input and passages textarea have associated labels; state announced via role=status aria-live. No content images without alt (text/table only). Conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names derive as auto-checks.

Browser matrix: desktop (1280×800) measured 0px overflow; mobile (360×740 DPR3) initially had 65px overflow from the at-a-glance table's long unbreakable model id — FIXED in-place with a scoped .inside-table { overflow-wrap:anywhere } style; re-measured 0px on the main page AND all four sub-pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model). The wide code example scrolls inside its own overflow-x:auto <pre>, not the page. Screenshots reviewed light+dark on both classes. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify/jina-reranker-v2/{desktop,mobile}-{light,dark}.png
rubric.notes: Targeted overflow fix, re-measured 0px across main + all sub-pages.

rubric.evidence: All cross-encoder inference runs in a module Web Worker; the page only renders the returned ranking, so the interaction path stays off the main thread and INP stays low. Batched single forward pass over all pairs. 0 console errors during the run.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy JS off the interaction path so INP stays low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the cross-encoder batch forward pass runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only renders the ranked list + before/after view.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: models/jina-reranker-v2/worker.js runs the model; rerank.js renders; browser run had 0 console errors and stayed responsive

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference is entirely off the main thread in a module worker.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid grids that wrap; avoid fixed widths; wide content scrolls in its own container; long unbreakable tokens must wrap so tables don't force page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .ba-wrap/.ct-wrap use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax(240px,1fr)); result rows wrap long CJK/accented passages; scoped .inside-table { overflow-wrap:anywhere } added so the long model id wraps on mobile; the code <pre> scrolls internally.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: mobile overflow re-measured 0px on main + all 4 sub-pages after the fix

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear, associated labels.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the query <input> and passages <textarea> have associated <label>s; scenario chips and Rerank are real buttons; Enter reranks.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run panel markup; conformance buttons-have-names derives as pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; landmarks + heading order.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #runStatus, labelled controls, keyboard-operable buttons, header/footer landmarks.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a jina-reranker-v2 code-search multi-model demo pairing it with a code-embedding retriever (e.g. jina-embeddings-v2-code / CodeBERT) for a full two-stage code retrieval pipeline.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for jina-reranker-v2 (jinaai/jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual, Jina AI's second-generation multilingual cross-encoder — 100+ languages PLUS code/function-calling, distinct from the v1 tiny English-only reranker and the BGE rerankers). Real in-browser WASM rerank verified in headless Chrome: the relevant passage was promoted from #6 to #1 across a mixed EN/ZH/ES corpus, 0 console errors; Node cross-checked the [N,1] logit shape, cross-lingual ranking, and correct CODE reranking (factorial snippet #1). Loads via AutoModelForSequenceClassification with an injected model_type (the ONNX config omits it) — documented honestly on the page, no remote code. Fresh-profile Download (~280 MB) + cached auto-init (no Load button) both verified. A mobile at-a-glance overflow was fixed in-place and re-measured 0px on main + all sub-pages; light+dark reviewed. License CC-BY-NC-4.0 (research/non-commercial) recorded on the page and catalogue.
